title: After death of 11-year-old Lyhanna, French public invited to courthouse voice anger at justice system
subhead: A courthouse in the Paris region opened its doors to the public on Wednesday after the fallout over the death of 11-year-old schoolgirl Lyhanna, which has raised questions about possible failings in the justice system. Around 60 people gathered to learn more and to share their views.

title: Vance postpones trip to Switzerland for Iran talks
Ukrainian drone assault on Moscow undermines Putin's promises of victory
Online betting ads are everywhere during the World Cup, but a law could soon ban them during matches
subhead: On June 18, Pascal Chèvremont was appointed the new head of the French National Gambling Authority (AFJ) as online betting offers continue to multiply during the World Cup. A draft bill, to be examined on June 29 at the Assemblée Nationale, could ban advertising for them during matches.

title: Wealthy taxpayers clean out their holdings, keeping their homes, sports cars and gold bars safe
subhead: The new 20% tax on 'luxury' assets held in personal holdings is prompting owners to quickly remove them. As a result, the measure risks bringing in almost nothing.

title: Mont-Saint-Michel will no longer be managed by France's National Monuments Center
subhead: Listed as a UNESCO World Heritage site, Mont-Saint-Michel will now be managed exclusively by the public industrial and commercial establishment (EPIC). The iconic Norman landmark was in a months-long standoff between the two operators, EPIC and the National Monuments Center (CMN), which had been jointly managing the site.

title: 'In France, 50% of buildings are not adapted to heatwaves'
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, Julien Hans, a specialist in building quality, said that around 10 million buildings in France are not adapted to high temperatures, even in cases where they were recently renovated.

'Incidents multiply in the Baltic Sea, where the Russian navy 'now operates in a completely overt manner'
subhead: After a Russian frigate fired warning shots at a civilian yacht in the English Channel on June 16, Rear Admiral Johan Norlén, chief of staff of the Swedish navy, describes an increasingly tense climate in Baltic waters.

title: French security replaces Palantir with domestic firm ChapsVision, a complicated choice
subhead: Used since the 2015 terrorist attacks, the controversial American company's data analysis software is set to be gradually replaced by that of a French provider. The long-awaited decision presents a significant technological challenge.

title: From insider trading in France to an American cell phone: How Spain tracked down former prime minister Zapatero
subhead: Judicial cooperation among several countries, including France and Switzerland, has helped uncover an international network relying on 'opaque financial channels,' which were allegedly used, among other things, to pay the former Socialist leader.
